docs.title: kaicrit — CriticMarkup for VS Code
docs.text: kaicrit adds CriticMarkup editing support to VS Code: insert change markers, navigate between them, and accept or reject changes — all without leaving the editor.

docs.title: What is CriticMarkup?
docs.text: CriticMarkup is a plain-text standard for tracking changes and inline comments. It works in any text file using simple bracket syntax. The full specification is available at criticmarkup.com.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Download the latest kaicrit-*.vsix from the Releases page, then install it:

code --install-extension kaicrit-*.vsix

The extension activates automatically on VS Code startup.

docs.title: Keybindings
docs.text: All keybindings are active when the editor has text focus. Commands are also available via the Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P) under the CriticMarkup category.

docs.title: Insert
docs.text:
Wrap a selection before triggering to mark existing text; trigger without a selection to insert empty markers at the cursor.

Action Keybinding Insert Deletion Alt+K Alt+D Insert Addition Alt+K Alt+A Insert Substitution Alt+K Alt+S Insert Highlight Alt+K Alt+H Insert Comment Alt+K Alt+C

docs.title: Navigate
docs.text: Action Keybinding Next Change Alt+K Alt+↓ Previous Change Alt+K Alt+↑ First Change Alt+K Alt+Home Last Change Alt+K Alt+End

docs.title: Accept / Reject
docs.text: Action Keybinding Accept Change at Cursor Alt+A Reject Change at Cursor Alt+R Accept All Changes Alt+K Enter Reject All Changes Alt+K Backspace

docs.title: Markup Types
docs.text: kaicrit supports all five CriticMarkup types. Each type is rendered with a distinct color; marker characters are visually de-emphasized.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: Type Syntax Purpose Deletion {--deleted text--} Mark text for removal Addition {++added text++} Mark text for insertion Substitution {~~old~>new~~} Mark a replacement pair Highlight {==highlighted text==} Mark text for attention Comment {>>comment text<<} Inline annotation

docs.title: Accept / Reject Semantics
docs.text:
Accepting or rejecting a change strips the markup and resolves the content:

Type Accept Reject Deletion {--T--} removes T keeps T Addition {++T++} keeps T removes T Substitution {~~O~>N~~} keeps N keeps O Highlight {==T==} keeps T (strips markers) keeps T (strips markers) Comment {>>T<<} removes entirely removes entirely

Accept All / Reject All apply all resolutions atomically in a single edit.
